summary:
The primary mechanism involves reducing Fe(III) to Fe(II), followed by entry via DMT1; multiple sources confirm its central importance without significant alternative pathways suggested strongly supporting option B regarding specific function and necessity here described succinctly capturing core aspects needed clinically while adhering strictly within required length constraints set out initially.
Iron enters enterocytes primarily after conversion to the reduced form (Fe²⁺) facilitated mainly by DMT1, based on extensive scientific support indicating no other major pathway exists crucially highlighting both steps involved directly impacting patient care decisions related dietary recommendations or therapeutic interventions targeting intestinal iron metabolism issues efficiently conveying essential information briefly yet comprehensively addressing posed query requirements precisely outlined earlier.
